commit.message:
[Agent] Cheats: Online cheat code lookup from libretro database (#2565)

* feat: add online cheat code lookup from libretro database

- Add `CheatOnlineLookup` actor that queries GitHub-hosted libretro
  cheat database (.cht files) when local results are empty
- Strategy 1: direct raw URL with sanitised title; Strategy 2:
  GitHub directory-listing API with Dice-coefficient fuzzy matching
- 24 h disk cache + in-memory cache to respect rate limits
- Add `isOnlineResult` flag to `CheatDatabaseEntry` for UI badging
- Expose `CheatDatabase.searchCheatsOnline` as a thin wrapper
- iOS/tvOS search views gain "Search Online" / "Also Search Online"
  buttons (shown only when `cheatsOnlineLookup` feature flag is on)
- Globe icon badge on individual online results for trust transparency
- Add `cheatsOnlineLookup` to `PVFeature` enum and
  `PVFeatureFlagsManager`

Closes #2548

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot review issues in cheat online lookup

- Fix logical bug in diceSimilarity: use && instead of || so that
  an empty bigram set short-circuits to 0 without dividing by zero
- Show onlineErrorMessage in an alert when online cheat search fails
  (previously set but never displayed to the user)
- Simplify duplicate ternary in emptyStateView: both arms of
  filterText.isEmpty ? "magnifyingglass" : "magnifyingglass" were
  identical; replace with the literal string directly

Part of #2548

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cheats): address Copilot review issues in online cheat lookup

- CheatOnlineLookup: propagate errors from both fetch strategies instead
  of swallowing them with try?; throw dedicated missingSystemIdentifier
  error when no system ID is provided
- CheatOnlineLookup: replace truncated disk cache filenames with FNV-1a
  hash to eliminate collision risk and filesystem-unfriendly characters
- CheatOnlineLookup: fix parseCht to iterate union of descs and codes
  indices so entries with a code but no description are not dropped
- CheatOnlineLookup: replace Set-based bigrams with a frequency
  dictionary for a standards-compliant Sørensen-Dice multiset coefficient
- CheatOnlineLookup: add real cache eviction (oldest entry removed when
  maxMemoryCacheEntries=50 is exceeded); fix misleading LRU comment
- iOSCheatsView: add !isOnlineSearching guard to both online search
  buttons to prevent concurrent duplicate requests
- iOSCheatsView: gate "Search Online" on non-empty gameSystemIdentifier
- iOSCheatsView: set hasSearchedOnline only on success; use defer to
  clear isOnlineSearching on all exit paths
- iOSCheatsView: remove !isOnlineSearching from emptyStateView condition
  so the ProgressView overlay is reachable during in-flight searches
- TVOSCheatsView: same hasSearchedOnline/defer fix as iOS
- TVOSCheatsView: surface online errors via errorMessage for user feedback
- TVOSCheatsView: remove unreachable if isOnlineSearching branch inside
  button label guarded by !isOnlineSearching in the outer condition

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update PVUI/Sources/PVUIBase/Cheats/TVOSCheatsView.swift

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update PVLibrary/Sources/PVLibrary/Cheat/CheatOnlineLookup.swift

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address Copilot review issues in CheatOnlineLookup and tvOS view

- fetchRawCht: treat HTTP 404 as "not found" (return nil) rather than
  throwing, so a guessed URL miss does not incorrectly surface as an
  error when strategy 2 also finds no match for the title
- loadDiskCache: return (fetchedAt, entries) tuple so disk-cache hits
  populate the in-memory cache with the original timestamp, preventing
  TTL extension beyond the intended 24-hour window
- TVOSCheatsView: gate "Also Search Online" bottom bar on a non-empty
  gameSystemIdentifier, matching the empty-state button guard already
  in place (avoids a guaranteed-failure path when system ID is unknown)
- Update searchCheats docstring to accurately describe throwing behaviour

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update PVUI/Sources/PVUIBase/Cheats/TVOSCheatsView.swift

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address remaining Copilot review issues in cheats online lookup

- CheatOnlineLookup: throw for non-404 non-2xx GitHub API responses
- CheatOnlineLookup: propagate errors from fetchRawCht in dir-listing path
- CheatOnlineLookup: remove unused httpGet helper
- iOSCheatsView: gate Also Search Online on non-empty gameSystemIdentifier
- TVOSCheatsView: dedicated onlineErrorMessage for online-search failures
- CheatDatabaseEntry: clarify doc comments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(main): reduce complexity and function length

Extract main() into smaller helper functions to improve code quality:
- setupLogging(): Configure logrus formatter
- parseAndValidateFlags(): Handle help and version flags
- determineConfigDir(): Get and create config directory
- loadOrCreateConfig(): Load config and handle debug flag
- createToxClient(): Initialize Tox client
- runClientWithGracefulShutdown(): Handle signals and client lifecycle

Results:
- main() cyclomatic complexity: 14 → 2 (85.7% improvement)
- main() overall complexity: 19.2 → 3.1 (83.9% improvement)
- main() lines: 128 → 13 (89.8% reduction)
- All new functions have cyclomatic complexity ≤ 5 (target: ≤ 10)

Addresses ROADMAP.md Priority 1A item 1: Refactor main function.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: embed S3 bucket name into Lambda zip at deploy time

Lambda@Edge cannot use environment variables. Instead, the CI workflow
now generates config.js with the bucket name baked in before Terraform
packages the zip.

Changes:
- deploy-image-pipeline.yml: new 'Embed bucket config' step runs after
  AWS credentials are configured; derives account ID via sts, writes
  infra/image-transformer/config.js with IMAGE_BUCKET and BUCKET_REGION
- handler.js: require('./config') instead of process.env or event-based
  derivation; S3Client initialized once at module load with baked region
- .gitignore: exclude infra/image-transformer/config.js (generated file)

feat: invert token limits priority to .waza.yaml first #59 (#64)

Priority order:

Tests cover all four scenarios: waza-only, legacy-only, both-present
(.waza.yaml wins), and neither-present.

commit.message:
coverage: add 47 seeds + dict entries targeting 6 gap files

New seeds targeting coverage gaps:
- BPC: 6 RGB↔CMYK link pairs with BPC/luminance/subprofile ctrl bits
  (IccApplyBPC.cpp 30% → target)
- PCC: 12 v5 spectral profile pairs with differing viewing conditions
  (IccPcc.cpp 16%, IccMpeSpectral.cpp 31%)
- Matrix: 4 matrix-TRC → Lab pairs (IccMatrixMath.cpp 39%)
- NamedColor: 12 seeds with BPC/luminance/tetrahedral flags
- Sparse matrix: 3 profiles → deep_dump + profile fuzzers
  (IccSparseMatrix.cpp 26%)
- V5 spectral: 5 profiles → spectral/spectral_b/v5dspobs fuzzers
- PRMG: 1 profile → deep_dump + profile fuzzers (IccPrmg.cpp 0%)

Dictionary additions (8 fuzzer dicts):
- Sparse matrix type tokens (smat, uint8/16/float16/32/floatnum)
- Spectral MPE elements (emtx, iemx, eclt, rclt, eobs, robs)
- Spectral range values (380nm-780nm, 31/41/81 steps)
- PCC viewing conditions (D50, D65, 2°/10° observer)
- BPC tag tokens (bkpt, chad, wtpt, mwpt, mbpt)
- Embedded profile tokens (ICC5)
- PRMG gamut tags (prmg, rig0, rig2)

ramdisk-seed.sh: added Source 3.5 for per-fuzzer seed corpus dirs.

commit.message:
Rewrite all 15 modules as comprehensive self-learning articles

- Expanded from ~48 KB to ~337 KB of learning content (7x increase)
- Added explanatory prose, architecture diagrams (Mermaid), code examples
- Azure CLI, PowerShell, Bicep, and KQL examples throughout
- MkDocs Material formatting: admonitions, tabs, tables, code copy
- Cross-references to related labs and Microsoft Learn documentation
- Key Takeaways and References sections in every module

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(hot-reload): race condition, exact matching, cancel-and-restart, scroll preservation

Tier 1 fixes (bugs/correctness):
- compilationState race condition: SemaphoreSlim(1,1) serializes file
  change processing so two rapid saves can't corrupt mutable state
- FuzzySharp replaced with exact EndsWith suffix matching: prevents
  wrong method detours (e.g. getUser vs getUsers had high Fuzz.Ratio)
- #load fallback removed: parse failures now broadcastCompilationFailed
  instead of dangerously re-executing module-level side effects
- NonBlockingRun guard: skip string rewriting when hotReload=true to
  avoid conflict with DevReloadInjector's Harmony patches

Tier 2 fixes (DX improvements):
- Cancel-and-restart on rapid saves: per-file CancellationTokenSource
  dictionary cancels in-flight eval when newer save arrives, preventing
  intermediate error flashes
- Scroll position preservation: saves window.scrollY to sessionStorage
  before DevReload SSE triggers page reload, restores on load
- Prefer highest assembly version: resolveAssembly collects all
  candidates and sortByDescending version instead of first-match

Tests: 57 hot reload tests (11 new: exact matching, NonBlockingRun
guard, version resolution). All DevReload tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add comprehensive client package tests (Step 5.1)

- Created client/client_test.go with tests for:
  * SendMessage validation (empty, too long, UTF-8 byte counting)
  * Friend map concurrent access (add, get, update, delete)
  * GetFriend method
  * Message type handling (normal vs action)
  * Friend struct status values
  * Client initialization
  * Transfer key generation
  * Conference struct

- Created client/fifo_test.go with tests for:
  * FIFO path generation (global, friend, conference)
  * Tox ID parsing and validation (76-char hex)
  * Message type detection (/me prefix)
  * FIFO constants and permissions
  * Input parsing patterns
  * Friend removal confirmation logic
  * Path joining behavior

- All tests pass with race detection
- Zero regression in complexity, duplication, or doc coverage
- Updated PLAN.md to mark Step 5.1 complete
- Documented Step 4.6 as blocked due to toxcore API limitations

commit.message:
fix(acp): protocolVersion must be number 1, not string '2025-01'

The ACP server returns an error when protocolVersion is a string.
This caused initialize to fail silently, hanging all queries.
Fix: send protocolVersion: 1 (number) as the ACP spec requires.

commit.message:
Add workspace route edge-case tests for upload and file size limits

Add 4 new tests covering previously untested paths:
- Upload with invalid filename (. or ..) returns 400
- Upload exceeding MAX_FILE_WRITE_BYTES returns 413 and cleans up
- PUT /workspace/file with oversized content returns 413
- Upload to non-directory path falls back to parent directory

Workspace route coverage: 87% -> 88%. Total: 318 tests passing.

commit.message:
Decompose GameSession god object with IMessageHandler interface and 5 domain handlers

Extract domain-specific message handling from GameSession into dedicated
handler classes that implement the new IMessageHandler interface:

- CombatHandler: TARGET_LOCK, TARGET_UNLOCK, MODULE_ACTIVATE, MODULE_DEACTIVATE
- StationHandler: DOCK_REQUEST, UNDOCK_REQUEST, REPAIR_REQUEST
- MovementHandler: WARP_REQUEST, APPROACH, ORBIT, STOP
- ScannerHandler: SCAN_START, SCAN_STOP, ANOMALY_LIST
- MissionHandler: MISSION_LIST, ACCEPT_MISSION, ABANDON_MISSION, MISSION_PROGRESS

GameSession now delegates to handlers via a canHandle()/handle() loop,
keeping only core session management (CONNECT, DISCONNECT, INPUT_MOVE, CHAT).
System injection methods are preserved on GameSession and forwarded to the
appropriate handler, maintaining backward compatibility.

This addresses SPAGHETTI_CODE_AUDIT.md Issue 3, Step 3.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add alternate transport support (TCP, Tor, I2P foundation)

Implements Step 4.5 from PLAN.md with partial completion due to toxcore API limitations.

Changes:
- Add TransportConfig struct with TCP, Tor, and I2P configuration options
- Refactor initTox() into smaller helper functions (complexity: 9 → 2, 77.8% improvement)
- Add transport validation to enforce mutually exclusive Tor/I2P
- Create transport_status global FIFO to expose active transport type
- Set sensible defaults (all alternate transports disabled by default)
- Disable UDP automatically when Tor or I2P is enabled

Implementation notes:
- TCP port configuration works via toxcore Options.TCPPort
- Tor/I2P proxy integration awaits toxcore Proxy API documentation
- All tests pass with race detection
- Overall complexity trend: improving (Quality Score: 18.2/100)

commit.message:
Fix: Remove GSEexplore from renv.lock to prevent manifest deployment conflict

GSEexplore is a local development package being loaded directly in app.R.
When included in renv.lock, Posit Publisher generates manifest.json with GitHub fields
that Posit Connect Cloud cannot properly resolve. This causes deployment failure.

Solution: Remove GSEexplore from renv.lock but keep all its dependencies:
- config, golem, shiny, Biobase, GEOquery, limma, sva, pheatmap, ggplot2, plotly, DT

The local package will be loaded via library(GSEexplore) in app.R after
the Shiny package loads, without requiring installation from manifest.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update .github/workflows/deploy-staging.yml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Complete JUnit 4 to JUnit 5 migration (#5894)

* Complete JUnit 4 to JUnit 5 migration

Remove all remaining JUnit 4 artifacts from the codebase:

- Delete 5 JUnit 4 test files (JUnit 5 equivalents already exist)
- Delete 5 deprecated JUnit 4 base test classes (BaseContextSensitiveTest,
  BaseContextMockTest, BaseModuleContextSensitiveTest,
  BaseWebContextSensitiveTest, BaseModuleWebContextSensitiveTest)

  org.junit.internal.matchers.TypeSafeMatcher)
- Update TestTypeFilter (production code) to reference jupiter base classes
- Update Javadoc @see references to point to jupiter equivalents

Add doc/JUNIT5_MIGRATION.md with step-by-step instructions covering:
- Base class replacements (old → jupiter equivalents)

- Assertion parameter order changes (message moves to last)

- POM dependency updates

* Add comprehensive test coverage for ThreadSafeCircularFifoQueue

Add 16 new tests to close coverage gaps identified in code review:

- Drain-cycle tests: fill then drain via element/remove and peek/poll
- Sequential offer/add with verification after each
- Heterogeneous element types (String, Integer, Double, Long, Short)
- Object contract: equals-self, equals-null, hashCode repeatability
- Iterator edge cases: remove-before-next (ISE), double-remove (ISE),
  drain all via iterator remove, next beyond end (NSEE)
- Remove/removeAll with non-existent elements returns false
- Contains null returns false
- toArray with exact-size array
- Serialization of empty queue

Total: 50 tests (was 34)

commit.message:
feat: spec-correct direct and indirect eval (closes #302) (#342)

Implement spec-aligned eval() with direct vs indirect forms per
ECMAScript §19.2.1.1:

- Direct eval (eval('code')): shares the caller's variable environment
  so var declarations are hoisted into the enclosing scope.
- Indirect eval ((0, eval)('code')): runs in a fresh global scope,
  completely isolated from the call site.
- Strict-mode eval: creates its own variable environment; var
  declarations do not leak into the caller's scope.

Implementation details:
- New CallDirectEval bytecode opcode emitted when the callee is the
  bare 'eval' identifier; runtime Rc::ptr_eq check confirms the callee
  is still the built-in eval function before applying direct semantics.
- BytecodeGenerator::compile_eval_program() compiles var declarations
  as StaGlobal (instead of local registers) so they persist in the
  shared global_env.
- eval registered as a NativeFunction in install_globals for indirect
  access via (0, eval)() or var e = eval; e().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement conference/group chat support (partial)

Add basic conference/group chat functionality via FIFO interface:

- Added Conference struct and tracking in Client
- Added conference_in global FIFO to create conferences
- Created per-conference directories with text_in and invite_in FIFOs
- Implemented conference message sending and friend invitations
- Added monitoring for conference FIFOs
- Added ConferenceDir/ConferenceFIFOPath helper methods

Limitations (blocked by toxcore API gaps):
- No conference message receive callbacks (OnConferenceMessage)
- No conference invite callbacks (OnConferenceInvite)
- No conference member enumeration APIs

When toxcore adds these callbacks, receiving messages and managing
invites can be completed.

Tests: All tests pass with race detection
Complexity: All new functions ≤7 cyclomatic (well below threshold of 10)

Addresses: PLAN.md Step 4.3

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
